L. 111–148, title III, § 3205(d)124 Stat. 458July 15, 2008Mar. 23, 2010“(2).—Authority to operate but no service area expansion for dual snps that do not meet certain requirementsNotwithstanding subsection (f) of section 1859 of the Social Security Act (), during the period beginning on , and ending on , in the case of a specialized Medicare Advantage plan for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) of such section, as amended by this section, that does not meet the requirement described in subsection (f)(3)(D) of such section, the Secretary of Health and Human Services—42 U.S.C. 1395w–28January 1, 2010December 31, 2012“(A)shall permit such plan to be offered under part C of title XVIII of such Act [ et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 1395w–21“(B)shall not permit an expansion of the service area of the plan under such part C.“(3).—Resources for state medicaid agenciesThe Secretary of Health and Human Services shall provide for the designation of appropriate staff and resources that can address State inquiries with respect to the coordination of State and Federal policies for specialized MA plans for special needs individuals described in section 1859(b)(6)(B)(ii) of the Social Security Act (), as amended by this section.42 U.S.C. 1395w–28(b)(6)(B)(ii)“(4).—No requirement for contractNothing in the provisions of, or amendments made by, this subsection [amending this section] shall require a State to enter into a contract with a Medicare Advantage organization with respect to a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals described in section 1859(b)(6)(B)(ii) of the Social Security Act (), as amended by this section.”42 U.S.C. 1395w–28(b)(6)(B)(ii)

children.heading: Definitions relating to Medicare+Choice organizations
children.label: a Definitions relating to Medicare+Choice organizations

children.heading: Medicare+Choice organization
children.label: 1 Medicare+Choice organization
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice organization” means a public or private entity that is certified under as meeting the requirements and standards of this part for such an organization.section 1395w–26 of this title

children.heading: Provider-sponsored organization
children.label: 2 Provider-sponsored organization
children.text: The term “provider-sponsored organization” is defined in .section 1395w–25(d)(1) of this title

children.heading: Definitions relating to Medicare+Choice plans
children.label: b Definitions relating to Medicare+Choice plans

children.heading: Medicare+Choice plan
children.label: 1 Medicare+Choice plan
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice plan” means health benefits coverage offered under a policy, contract, or plan by a Medicare+Choice organization pursuant to and in accordance with a contract under .section 1395w–27 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: reimburses hospitals, physicians, and other providers at a rate determined by the plan on a fee-for-service basis without placing the provider at financial risk;

children.label: B
children.text: does not vary such rates for such a provider based on utilization relating to such provider; and

children.label: C
children.text: does not restrict the selection of providers among those who are lawfully authorized to provide the covered services and agree to accept the terms and conditions of payment established by the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: provides reimbursement for at least the items and services described in in a year but only after the enrollee incurs countable expenses (as specified under the plan) equal to the amount of an annual deductible (described in subparagraph (B));section 1395w–22(a)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: counts as such expenses (for purposes of such deductible) at least all amounts that would have been payable under parts A and B, and that would have been payable by the enrollee as deductibles, coinsurance, or copayments, if the enrollee had elected to receive benefits through the provisions of such parts; and

children.label: I
children.text: 100 percent of such expenses, or

children.label: II
children.text: 100 percent of the amounts that would have been paid (without regard to any deductibles or coinsurance) under parts A and B with respect to such expenses,

children.label: i
children.text: for contract year 1999 shall be not more than $6,000; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for a subsequent contract year shall be not more than the maximum amount of such deductible for the previous contract year under this subparagraph increased by the national per capita Medicare+Choice growth percentage under for the year.section 1395w–23(c)(6) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: that has a network of providers that have agreed to a contractually specified reimbursement for covered benefits with the organization offering the plan;

children.label: B
children.text: that provides for reimbursement for all covered benefits regardless of whether such benefits are provided within such network of providers; and

children.label: C
children.text: the service area of which is one or more entire MA regions.

children.heading: MA local plan
children.label: 5 MA local plan
children.text: The term “MA local plan” means an MA plan that is not an MA regional plan.

children.heading: Specialized MA plans for special needs individuals
children.label: 6 Specialized MA plans for special needs individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “specialized MA plan for special needs individuals” means an MA plan that exclusively serves special needs individuals (as defined in subparagraph (B)) and that, as of , meets the applicable requirements of paragraph (2), (3), or (4) of subsection (f), as the case may be.January 1, 2010

children.label: i
children.text: is institutionalized (as defined by the Secretary);

children.label: ii
children.text: is entitled to medical assistance under a State plan under subchapter XIX; or

children.label: I
children.text: before , have one or more comorbid and medically complex chronic conditions that are substantially disabling or life threatening, have a high risk of hospitalization or other significant adverse health outcomes, and require specialized delivery systems across domains of care; andJanuary 1, 2022

children.label: II
children.text: on or after , have one or more comorbid and medically complex chronic conditions that is life threatening or significantly limits overall health or function, have a high risk of hospitalization or other adverse health outcomes, and require intensive care coordination and that is listed under subsection (f)(9)(A).January 1, 20221So in original. Probably should be “that are life threatening or significantly limit”.1

children.heading: Other references to other terms
children.label: c Other references to other terms

children.heading: Medicare+Choice eligible individual
children.label: 1 Medicare+Choice eligible individual
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice eligible individual” is defined in .section 1395w–21(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Medicare+Choice payment area
children.label: 2 Medicare+Choice payment area
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice payment area” is defined in .section 1395w–23(d) of this title

children.heading: National per capita Medicare+Choice growth percentage
children.label: 3 National per capita Medicare+Choice growth percentage
children.text: The “national per capita Medicare+Choice growth percentage” is defined in .section 1395w–23(c)(6) of this title

children.heading: Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium; Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium
children.label: 4 Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium; Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium
children.text: The terms “Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium” and “Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium” are defined in .section 1395w–24(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: MA local area
children.label: 5 MA local area
children.text: The term “MA local area” is defined in .section 1395w–23(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Coordinated acute and long-term care benefits under Medicare+Choice plan
children.label: d Coordinated acute and long-term care benefits under Medicare+Choice plan
children.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed as preventing a State from coordinating benefits under a medicaid plan under subchapter XIX with those provided under a Medicare+Choice plan in a manner that assures continuity of a full-range of acute care and long-term care services to poor elderly or disabled individuals eligible for benefits under this subchapter and under such plan.

children.heading: Restriction on enrollment for certain Medicare+Choice plans
children.label: e Restriction on enrollment for certain Medicare+Choice plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a Medicare+Choice religious fraternal benefit society plan described in paragraph (2), notwithstanding any other provision of this part to the contrary and in accordance with regulations of the Secretary, the society offering the plan may restrict the enrollment of individuals under this part to individuals who are members of the church, convention, or group described in paragraph (3)(B) with which the society is affiliated.

children.heading: Medicare+Choice religious fraternal benefit society plan described
children.label: 2 Medicare+Choice religious fraternal benefit society plan described
A
children.text: is offered by a religious fraternal benefit society described in paragraph (3) only to members of the church, convention, or group described in paragraph (3)(B); and

children.label: B
children.text: permits all such members to enroll under the plan without regard to health status-related factors.

children.label: A
children.text: is described in section 501(c)(8) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and is exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such Act;

children.label: B
children.text: is affiliated with, carries out the tenets of, and shares a religious bond with, a church or convention or association of churches or an affiliated group of churches;

children.label: C
children.text: offers, in addition to a Medicare+ÐChoice religious fraternal benefit society plan, health coverage to individuals not entitled to benefits under this subchapter who are members of such church, convention, or group; and

children.label: D
children.text: does not impose any limitation on membership in the society based on any health status-related factor.

children.heading: Payment adjustment
children.label: 4 Payment adjustment
children.text: Under regulations of the Secretary, in the case of individuals enrolled under this part under a Medicare+Choice religious fraternal benefit society plan described in paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide for such adjustment to the payment amounts otherwise established under as may be appropriate to assure an appropriate payment level, taking into account the actuarial characteristics and experience of such individuals.section 1395w–24 of this title

children.heading: Requirements regarding enrollment in specialized MA plans for special needs individuals
children.label: f Requirements regarding enrollment in specialized MA plans for special needs individuals

children.heading: Requirements for enrollment
children.label: 1 Requirements for enrollment
children.text: In the case of a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(6)), notwithstanding any other provision of this part and in accordance with regulations of the Secretary, the plan may restrict the enrollment of individuals under the plan to individuals who are within one or more classes of special needs individuals.

children.label: i
children.text: using a State assessment tool of the State in which the individual resides; and

children.label: ii
children.text: by an entity other than the organization offering the plan.

children.label: B
children.text: The plan meets the requirements described in paragraph (5).

children.label: C
children.text: If applicable, the plan meets the requirement described in paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: Each individual that enrolls in the plan on or after , is a special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii).January 1, 20102So in original. Probably should be “individual”.2

children.label: B
children.text: The plan meets the requirements described in paragraph (5).

children.label: i
children.text: the benefits and cost-sharing protections that the individual is entitled to under the State Medicaid program under subchapter XIX; and

children.label: ii
children.text: which of such benefits and cost-sharing protections are covered under the plan.

children.label: D
children.text: The plan has a contract with the State Medicaid agency to provide benefits, or arrange for benefits to be provided, for which such individual is entitled to receive as medical assistance under subchapter XIX. Such benefits may include long-term care services consistent with State policy.

children.label: E
children.text: If applicable, the plan meets the requirement described in paragraph (7).

children.label: F
children.text: The plan meets the requirements applicable under paragraph (8).

children.heading: Additional requirements for severe or disabling chronic condition SNPS
children.label: 4 Additional requirements for severe or disabling chronic condition SNPS
A
children.text: Each individual that enrolls in the plan on or after , is a special needs individual described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(iii).January 1, 2010

children.label: B
children.text: The plan meets the requirements described in paragraph (5).

children.label: C
children.text: If applicable, the plan meets the requirement described in paragraph (7).

children.heading: Care management requirements for all SNPs
children.label: 5 Care management requirements for all SNPs
i
children.text: have in place an evidenced-based model of care with appropriate networks of providers and specialists; and

children.label: I
children.text: conduct an initial assessment and an annual reassessment of the individual’s physical, psychosocial, and functional needs;

children.label: II
children.text: develop a plan, in consultation with the individual as feasible, that identifies goals and objectives, including measurable outcomes as well as specific services and benefits to be provided; and

children.label: III
children.text: use an interdisciplinary team in the management of care.

children.heading: Improvements to care management requirements for severe or disabling chronic condition SNPs
children.label: B Improvements to care management requirements for severe or disabling chronic condition SNPs
i
children.text: The interdisciplinary team under subparagraph (A)(ii)(III) includes a team of providers with demonstrated expertise, including training in an applicable specialty, in treating individuals similar to the targeted population of the plan.

children.label: ii
children.text: Requirements developed by the Secretary to provide face-to-face encounters with individuals enrolled in the plan not less frequently than on an annual basis.

children.label: iii
children.text: As part of the model of care under clause (i) of subparagraph (A), the results of the initial assessment and annual reassessment under clause (ii)(I) of such subparagraph of each individual enrolled in the plan are addressed in the individual’s individualized care plan under clause (ii)(II) of such subparagraph.

children.label: iv
children.text: As part of the annual evaluation and approval of such model of care, the Secretary shall take into account whether the plan fulfilled the previous year’s goals (as required under the model of care).

children.label: v
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a minimum benchmark for each element of the model of care of a plan. The Secretary shall only approve a plan’s model of care under this paragraph if each element of the model of care meets the minimum benchmark applicable under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Transition and exception regarding restriction on enrollment
children.label: 6 Transition and exception regarding restriction on enrollment
i
children.text: a Medicare Advantage plan that is not a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(6)); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the original medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B.

children.label: i
children.text: is enrolled under a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(6)); and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not within the 1 or more of the classes of special needs individuals to which enrollment under the plan is restricted to.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for an exception to the transition described in subparagraph (A) for a limited period of time for individuals enrolled under a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) who are no longer eligible for medical assistance under subchapter XIX.

children.heading: Timeline for initial transition
children.label: D Timeline for initial transition
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that applicable individuals enrolled in a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(6)) prior to , are transitioned to a plan or the program described in subparagraph (A) by not later than .January 1, 2010January 1, 2013

children.heading: Authority to require special needs plans be NCQA approved
children.label: 7 Authority to require special needs plans be NCQA approved
children.text: For 2012 and subsequent years, the Secretary shall require that a Medicare Advantage organization offering a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals be approved by the National Committee for Quality Assurance (based on standards established by the Secretary).

children.label: i
children.text: a uniform process for disseminating to State Medicaid agencies information under this subchapter impacting contracts between such agencies and such plans under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: basic resources for States interested in exploring such plans as a platform for integration, such as a model contract or other tools to achieve those goals.

children.heading: Unified grievances and appeals process
children.label: B Unified grievances and appeals process

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish procedures, to the extent feasible as determined by the Secretary, unifying grievances and appeals procedures under sections 1395w–22(f), 1395w–22(g), 1396a(a)(3), 1396a(a)(5), and 1396u–2(b)(4) of this title for items and services provided by specialized MA plans for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) under this subchapter and subchapter XIX. With respect to items and services described in the preceding sentence, procedures established under this clause shall apply in place of otherwise applicable grievances and appeals procedures. The Secretary shall solicit comment in developing such procedures from States, plans, beneficiaries and their representatives, and other relevant stakeholders.April 1, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: adopt the provisions for the enrollee that are most protective for the enrollee and, to the extent feasible as determined by the Secretary, are compatible with unified timeframes and consolidated access to external review under an integrated process;

children.label: II
children.text: take into account differences in State plans under subchapter XIX to the extent necessary;

children.label: III
children.text: be easily navigable by an enrollee; and

children.label: IV
children.text: include the elements described in clause (iii), as applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: Single written notification of all applicable grievances and appeal rights under this subchapter and subchapter XIX. For purposes of this subparagraph, the Secretary may waive the requirements under when the specialized MA plan covers items or services under this part or under subchapter XIX.section 1395w–22(g)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: Single pathways for resolution of any grievance or appeal related to a particular item or service provided by specialized MA plans for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) under this subchapter and subchapter XIX.

children.label: III
children.text: Notices written in plain language and available in a language and format that is accessible to the enrollee, including in non-English languages that are prevalent in the service area of the specialized MA plan.

children.label: IV
children.text: Unified timeframes for grievances and appeals processes, such as an individual’s filing of a grievance or appeal, a plan’s acknowledgment and resolution of a grievance or appeal, and notification of decisions with respect to a grievance or appeal.

children.label: V
children.text: Requirements for how the plan must process, track, and resolve grievances and appeals, to ensure beneficiaries are notified on a timely basis of decisions that are made throughout the grievance or appeals process and are able to easily determine the status of a grievance or appeal.

children.heading: Continuation of benefits pending appeal
children.label: iv Continuation of benefits pending appeal
children.text: The unified procedures under clause (i) shall, with respect to all benefits under parts A and B and subchapter XIX subject to appeal under such procedures, incorporate provisions under current law and implementing regulations that provide continuation of benefits pending appeal under this subchapter and subchapter XIX.

children.heading: Requirement for unified grievances and appeals
children.label: C Requirement for unified grievances and appeals
children.text: For 2021 and subsequent years, the contract of a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) with a State Medicaid agency under paragraph (3)(D) shall require the use of unified grievances and appeals procedures as described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: The specialized MA plan must meet the requirements of contracting with the State Medicaid agency described in paragraph (3)(D) in addition to coordinating long-term services and supports or behavioral health services, or both, by meeting an additional minimum set of requirements determined by the Secretary through the Federal Coordinated Health Care Office established under based on input from stakeholders, such as notifying the State in a timely manner of hospitalizations, emergency room visits, and hospital or nursing home discharges of enrollees, assigning one primary care provider for each enrollee, or sharing data that would benefit the coordination of items and services under this subchapter and the State plan under subchapter XIX. Such minimum set of requirements must be included in the contract of the specialized MA plan with the State Medicaid agency under such paragraph.section 1315b of this title

children.label: II
children.text: The specialized MA plan must meet the requirements of a fully integrated plan described in (other than the requirement that the plan have similar average levels of frailty, as determined by the Secretary, as the PACE program), or enter into a capitated contract with the State Medicaid agency to provide long-term services and supports or behavioral health services, or both.section 1395w–23(a)(1)(B)(iv)(II) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: In the case of a specialized MA plan that is offered by a parent organization that is also the parent organization of a Medicaid managed care organization providing long term services and supports or behavioral services under a contract under , the parent organization must assume clinical and financial responsibility for benefits provided under this subchapter and subchapter XIX with respect to any individual who is enrolled in both the specialized MA plan and the Medicaid managed care organization.section 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: Suspension of enrollment for failure to meet requirements during initial period
children.label: ii Suspension of enrollment for failure to meet requirements during initial period
children.text: During the period of plan years 2021 through 2025, if the Secretary determines that a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii) has failed to comply with clause (i), the Secretary may provide for the application against the Medicare Advantage organization offering the plan of the remedy described in in the same manner as the Secretary may apply such remedy, and in accordance with the same procedures as would apply, in the case of an MA organization determined by the Secretary to have engaged in conduct described in . If the Secretary applies such remedy to a Medicare Advantage organization under the preceding sentence, the organization shall submit to the Secretary (at a time, and in a form and manner, specified by the Secretary) information describing how the plan will come into compliance with clause (i).section 1395w–27(g)(2)(B) of this titlesection 1395w–27(g)(1) of this title

children.heading: Study and report to Congress
children.label: E Study and report to Congress
I
children.text: A comparison group of such plans that are described in subparagraph (D)(i)(I).

children.label: II
children.text: A comparison group of such plans that are described in subparagraph (D)(i)(II).

children.label: III
children.text: A comparison group of such plans operating within the Financial Alignment Initiative demonstration for the period for which such plan is so operating and the demonstration is in effect, and, in the case that an integration option that is not with respect to specialized MA plans for special needs individuals is established after the conclusion of the demonstration involved.

children.label: IV
children.text: A comparison group of such plans that are described in subparagraph (D)(i)(III).

children.label: V
children.text: A comparison group of MA plans, as feasible, not described in a previous subclause of this clause, with respect to the performance of such plans for enrollees who are special needs individuals described in subsection (b)(6)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Additional reports
children.label: ii Additional reports
children.text: Beginning with 2033 and every five years thereafter, the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission, in consultation with the Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission, shall conduct a study described in clause (i).

children.heading: List of conditions for clarification of the definition of a severe or disabling chronic conditions specialized needs individual
children.label: 9 List of conditions for clarification of the definition of a severe or disabling chronic conditions specialized needs individual
i
children.text: Conditions that meet the definition of a severe or disabling chronic condition under subsection (b)(6)(B)(iii) on or after .January 1, 2022

children.label: I
children.text: as a result of access to, and enrollment in, such a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals, individuals with such condition would have a reasonable expectation of slowing or halting the progression of the disease, improving health outcomes and decreasing overall costs for individuals diagnosed with such condition compared to available options of care other than through such a specialized MA plan for special needs individuals; or

children.label: II
children.text: have a low prevalence in the general population of beneficiaries under this subchapter or a disproportionally high per-beneficiary cost under this subchapter.

children.heading: Inclusion of certain conditions
children.label: B Inclusion of certain conditions
children.text: The conditions listed under subparagraph (A) shall include HIV/AIDS, end stage renal disease, and chronic and disabling mental illness.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: C Requirement
children.text: In establishing and updating the list under subparagraph (A), the panel shall take into account the availability of varied benefits, cost-sharing, and supplemental benefits under the model described in paragraph (2) of , including the expansion under paragraph (1) of such section.section 1395w–28(h) of this title

children.heading: Special rules for senior housing facility plans
children.label: g Special rules for senior housing facility plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a Medicare Advantage senior housing facility plan described in paragraph (2), notwithstanding any other provision of this part to the contrary and in accordance with regulations of the Secretary, the service area of such plan may be limited to a senior housing facility in a geographic area.

children.heading: Medicare Advantage senior housing facility plan described
children.label: 2 Medicare Advantage senior housing facility plan described
A
children.text: restricts enrollment of individuals under this part to individuals who reside in a continuing care retirement community (as defined in section 1395w–22()(4)(B) of this title);l

children.label: B
children.text: provides primary care services onsite and has a ratio of accessible physicians to beneficiaries that the Secretary determines is adequate;

children.label: C
children.text: provides transportation services for beneficiaries to specialty providers outside of the facility; and

children.label: D
children.text: has participated (as of ) in a demonstration project established by the Secretary under which such a plan was offered for not less than 1 year.December 31, 2009

children.heading: National testing of Medicare Advantage Value-Based Insurance Design model
children.label: h National testing of Medicare Advantage Value-Based Insurance Design model

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In implementing the Medicare Advantage Value-Based Insurance Design model that is being tested under , the Secretary shall revise the testing of the model under such section to cover, effective not later than , all States.section 1315a(b) of this titleJanuary 1, 2020

children.heading: Termination and modification provision not applicable untilJanuary 1, 2022
children.label: 2 Termination and modification provision not applicable untilJanuary 1, 2022
children.text: The provisions of shall apply to the Medicare Advantage Value-Based Insurance Design model, including such model as revised under paragraph (1), beginning , but shall not apply to such model, as so revised, prior to such date.section 1315a(b)(3)(B) of this titleJanuary 1, 2022

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds made available under to design, implement, and evaluate the Medicare Advantage Value-Based Insurance Design model, as revised under paragraph (1).section 1315a(f)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the referral by such plans of substantiated or suspicious activities, as defined by the Secretary, of a provider of services (including a prescriber) or supplier related to fraud, waste, and abuse for initiating or assisting investigations conducted by the eligible entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: data sharing among such MA plans, prescription drug plans, and the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: Providers of services and suppliers that have been referred pursuant to subparagraph (A)(i) during the previous 12-month period.

children.label: ii
children.text: Providers of services and suppliers who are the subject of an active exclusion under or who are subject to a suspension of payment under this subchapter pursuant to section 1395y() of this title or otherwise.section 1320a–7 of this titleo

children.label: iii
children.text: Providers of services and suppliers who are the subject of an active revocation of participation under this subchapter, including for not satisfying conditions of participation.

children.label: iv
children.text: In the case of such a plan that makes a referral under subparagraph (A)(i) through the portal (or other successor technology) with respect to activities of substantiated or suspicious activities of fraud, waste, or abuse of a provider of services (including a prescriber) or supplier, if such provider (including a prescriber) or supplier has been the subject of an administrative action under this subchapter or subchapter XI with respect to similar activities, a notification to such plan of such action so taken.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: C Rulemaking
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the Secretary shall, through rulemaking, specify what constitutes substantiated or suspicious activities of fraud, waste, and abuse, using guidance such as what is provided in the Medicare Program Integrity Manual 4.8. In carrying out this subsection, a fraud hotline tip (as defined by the Secretary) without further evidence shall not be treated as sufficient evidence for substantiated fraud, waste, or abuse.

children.heading: HIPAA compliant information only
children.label: D HIPAA compliant information only
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, communications may only occur if the communications are permitted under the Federal regulations (concerning the privacy of individually identifiable health information) promulgated under section 264(c) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996.

children.label: A
children.text: include administrative actions, pertinent information related to opioid overprescribing, and other data determined appropriate by the Secretary in consultation with stakeholders; and

children.label: B
children.text: be anonymized information submitted by plans without identifying the source of such information.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: 3 Clarification
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall preclude or otherwise affect referrals to the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services or other law enforcement entities.
